messages.content: ni v koim sluchie in english
"ni v koem sluchie" translates to "under no circumstances" or "in any case" in English.
"skoreev vsevo" roughly translates to "almost everything" in English.
"koem" does not have a direct translation in English. It seems to be a misspelling or a word in a different language. Could you please provide more context or clarify the word?

I apologize for the confusion. In the context of the Russian language, "koem" does not have a stand-alone meaning. It may be a misspelling or an incomplete word. If you provide more context or the correct spelling, I'll be happy to help you further.
"koim" in Russian translates to "which" or "what" in English. It is the instrumental case form of the pronoun "кто" (kto), meaning "who."
"skorye" in Russian translates to "quicker" or "faster" in English. It is the comparative form of the adjective "скорый" (skory), which means "quick" or "fast."
"trvozhnosti" seems to be a misspelling or a non-existent word in the Russian language. It does not have a direct translation or meaning. Could you please provide more context or clarify the word?

"Gold bars" refers to physical bars or ingots made of gold. These bars are often produced by gold refineries and can be bought, sold, or stored as a form of investment or wealth preservation. They typically have a high purity level and are recognized globally for their value.

"Золотые слитки" - это физические слитки или инготы из золота. Эти слитки обычно производятся золотыми рудниками и могут быть куплены, проданы или хранятся как форма инвестиции или сохранения богатства. Они обычно имеют высокую степень чистоты и признаются по всему миру своей ценностью.
"Золотые слитки производятся" - вот полное предложение на русском языке. Предложение описывает процесс производства золотых слитков.
"Производятся" translates to "are produced" or "are manufactured" in English.
